ReqMaster.system_instructions:
You are a Requisition Master agent handling job intake and requirements analysis.

Your responsibilities:
1. Retrieve and validate job posting details
2. Extract structured requirements (must-have vs nice-to-have)
3. Verify job is approved and open for sourcing
4. Identify hiring manager and team context
5. Set up screening criteria for candidate matching

When processing job requisitions:
- Validate the job ID exists and is in 'open' status
- Extract all required and preferred skills
- Note salary range and location requirements
- Identify urgency level and time-to-fill targets
- Flag any special requirements (clearance, certifications)

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
- If the position is time-sensitive or high priority, include the word "urgent" in your analysis
- If the role requires international hiring or global compliance, include "international" in your response
- Always clearly state the priority level (high/medium/low)

Output format: Provide structured job requirements ready for candidate sourcing.

tool_definitions.name: get_job_details
tool_definitions.description: Retrieves detailed information about a job posting including title, department, requirements, salary range, and hiring manager.

job_id.description: The job ID to retrieve (e.g., JOB-SWE-2025-001)
job_id.title: Job Id

tool_definitions.name: extract_requirements
tool_definitions.description: Extracts and structures the requirements from a job posting, separating must-have from nice-to-have skills.

job_id.description: The job ID to extract requirements from
job_id.title: Job Id

Source and evaluate candidates for JOB-CLOSED-2024-050.

Below I will present you a request.

Before we begin addressing the request, please answer the following pre-survey to the best of your ability.
Keep in mind that you are Ken Jennings-level with trivia, and Mensa-level with puzzles, so there should be
a deep well to draw from.

Here is the request:

Source and evaluate candidates for JOB-CLOSED-2024-050.

Here is the pre-survey:

    1. Please list any specific facts or figures that are GIVEN in the request itself. It is possible that
       there are none.
    2. Please list any facts that may need to be looked up, and WHERE SPECIFICALLY they might be found.
       In some cases, authoritative sources are mentioned in the request itself.
    3. Please list any facts that may need to be derived (e.g., via logical deduction, simulation, or computation)
    4. Please list any facts that are recalled from memory, hunches, well-reasoned guesses, etc.

When answering this survey, keep in mind that "facts" will typically be specific names, dates, statistics, etc.
Your answer should use headings:

    1. GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
    2. FACTS TO LOOK UP
    3. FACTS TO DERIVE
    4. EDUCATED GUESSES

DO NOT include any other headings or sections in your response. DO NOT list next steps or plans until asked to do so.

### 1. GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
- The job ID is JOB-CLOSED-2024-050.
- The status of the job is closed.

### 2. FACTS TO LOOK UP
- Confirmation of whether the job ID is in the system and any related details (e.g., job title, responsibilities, requirements).
- The reasons for closure of the job posting and if it can be reopened or if new postings will be created.

### 3. FACTS TO DERIVE
- The implications of sourcing candidates for a job that is marked as closed, including whether there are alternative positions or open roles that require similar candidates.
- The potential timelines and processes for reopening the job if necessary.

### 4. EDUCATED GUESSES
- It is likely that the job closure may be due to a successful hire or a change in organizational needs.

Source and evaluate candidates for JOB-CLOSED-2024-050.

Below I will present you a request.

Before we begin addressing the request, please answer the following pre-survey to the best of your ability.
Keep in mind that you are Ken Jennings-level with trivia, and Mensa-level with puzzles, so there should be
a deep well to draw from.

Here is the request:

Source and evaluate candidates for JOB-CLOSED-2024-050.

Here is the pre-survey:

    1. Please list any specific facts or figures that are GIVEN in the request itself. It is possible that
       there are none.
    2. Please list any facts that may need to be looked up, and WHERE SPECIFICALLY they might be found.
       In some cases, authoritative sources are mentioned in the request itself.
    3. Please list any facts that may need to be derived (e.g., via logical deduction, simulation, or computation)
    4. Please list any facts that are recalled from memory, hunches, well-reasoned guesses, etc.

When answering this survey, keep in mind that "facts" will typically be specific names, dates, statistics, etc.
Your answer should use headings:

    1. GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
    2. FACTS TO LOOK UP
    3. FACTS TO DERIVE
    4. EDUCATED GUESSES

DO NOT include any other headings or sections in your response. DO NOT list next steps or plans until asked to do so.

### 1. GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
- The job ID is JOB-CLOSED-2024-050.
- The status of the job is closed.

### 2. FACTS TO LOOK UP
- Confirmation of whether the job ID is in the system and any related details (e.g., job title, responsibilities, requirements).
- The reasons for closure of the job posting and if it can be reopened or if new postings will be created.

### 3. FACTS TO DERIVE
- The implications of sourcing candidates for a job that is marked as closed, including whether there are alternative positions or open roles that require similar candidates.
- The potential timelines and processes for reopening the job if necessary.

### 4. EDUCATED GUESSES
- It is likely that the job closure may be due to a successful hire or a change in organizational needs.

Fantastic. To address this request we have assembled the following team:

- ReqMaster: <no description, use name to identify the purpose of this participant>

Based on the team composition, and known and unknown facts, please devise a short bullet-point plan for addressing the
original request. Remember, there is no requirement to involve all team members. A team member's particular expertise
may not be needed for this task.

- **Validate Job ID**: Confirm the existence and closure status of JOB-CLOSED-2024-050 in the system.
- **Retrieve Job Details**: Gather specific information about the job, including title, responsibilities, requirements, and reasons for closure.
- **Assess Candidate Needs**: Determine if candidates are still required or if there are relevant open roles within the organization.
- **Consider Reopening**: Analyze the process for potentially reopening the job posting, including any necessary approvals.
- **Communicate Findings**: Present insights and recommendations regarding candidate sourcing or reopening to relevant stakeholders. 
- **Document Any Challenges**: Note any issues or barriers encountered during the evaluation process for future reference.

Please investigate the reasons for the job closure for JOB-CLOSED-2024-050 and determine if it can be reopened or if there are similar open roles available.

parts.response: Warning: Job 'JOB-CLOSED-2024-050' is not open. Current status: filled
Warning: Job 'JOB-CLOSED-2024-050' is not open. Current status: filled

parts.content:
The job with ID **JOB-CLOSED-2024-050** is currently marked as **filled**, meaning it has been closed for hiring. Here are the details regarding the closure:

### Reasons for Job Closure
- **Status**: Filled
- The role is no longer available for sourcing.

### Possibility to Reopen
- Generally, filled roles are not reopened unless there is a change in requirements or if a candidate falls through. If you want to pursue reopening this position, it would require communication with the hiring manager and potentially reconsideration of the role's necessity.

### Similar Open Roles
- I will need to check for any similar open roles. Please wait a moment.

parts.response: Error: Job ID 'JOB-OPEN-2024-001' not found in the system.
Error: Job ID 'JOB-OPEN-2024-002' not found in the system.
Error: Job ID 'JOB-OPEN-2024-003' not found in the system.
Error: Job ID 'JOB-OPEN-2024-004' not found in the system.

parts.content:
It appears that I am unable to retrieve information about other open roles at this time. If you have any specific roles or job IDs in mind to check for similar positions, please provide those, and I will assist you further.

### Summary:
- **Job Closure Reason**: The position JOB-CLOSED-2024-050 is filled.
- **Reopening**: Possible, but requires further discussion with the hiring manager.
- **Similar Open Roles**: Currently unable to locate similar positions.

If you need assistance with other queries, feel free to ask!

system_instructions:
You are a Requisition Master agent handling job intake and requirements analysis.

Your responsibilities:
1. Retrieve and validate job posting details
2. Extract structured requirements (must-have vs nice-to-have)
3. Verify job is approved and open for sourcing
4. Identify hiring manager and team context
5. Set up screening criteria for candidate matching

When processing job requisitions:
- Validate the job ID exists and is in 'open' status
- Extract all required and preferred skills
- Note salary range and location requirements
- Identify urgency level and time-to-fill targets
- Flag any special requirements (clearance, certifications)

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
- If the position is time-sensitive or high priority, include the word "urgent" in your analysis
- If the role requires international hiring or global compliance, include "international" in your response
- Always clearly state the priority level (high/medium/low)

Output format: Provide structured job requirements ready for candidate sourcing.

As a reminder, we are working to solve the following task:

Source and evaluate candidates for JOB-CLOSED-2024-050.

It is clear we are not making as much progress as we would like, but we may have learned something new.
Please rewrite the following fact sheet, updating it to include anything new we have learned that may be helpful.

Example edits can include (but are not limited to) adding new guesses, moving educated guesses to verified facts
if appropriate, etc. Updates may be made to any section of the fact sheet, and more than one section of the fact
sheet can be edited. This is an especially good time to update educated guesses, so please at least add or update
one educated guess or hunch, and explain your reasoning.

Here is the old fact sheet:

### 1. GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
- The job ID is JOB-CLOSED-2024-050.
- The status of the job is closed.

### 2. FACTS TO LOOK UP
- Confirmation of whether the job ID is in the system and any related details (e.g., job title, responsibilities, requirements).
- The reasons for closure of the job posting and if it can be reopened or if new postings will be created.

### 3. FACTS TO DERIVE
- The implications of sourcing candidates for a job that is marked as closed, including whether there are alternative positions or open roles that require similar candidates.
- The potential timelines and processes for reopening the job if necessary.

### 4. EDUCATED GUESSES
- It is likely that the job closure may be due to a successful hire or a change in organizational needs.

Here’s the updated fact sheet with the latest information regarding the job ID JOB-CLOSED-2024-050:

### 1. GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
- The job ID is **JOB-CLOSED-2024-050**.
- The status of the job is **closed**.

### 2. FACTS TO LOOK UP
- Confirmation of whether the job ID is in the system and any related details (e.g., job title, responsibilities, requirements).
- The reasons for closure of the job posting and whether it can be reopened or if new postings will be created.
- Explore the possibility of similar or related open positions within the organization.

### 3. FACTS TO DERIVE
- The implications of sourcing candidates for a job that is marked as closed, including **identifying alternative positions** or open roles that might require similar candidates.
- The potential timelines and processes for **reopening the job** if necessary, primarily influenced by hiring needs or the ability to reassess the role.

### 4. EDUCATED GUESSES
- It is likely that the job closure may be due to a **successful hire** or a **change in organizational needs**. This is supported by common hiring practices where positions are closed following a successful recruitment process.
- **New Guess**: There may be ongoing changes in hiring strategy, potentially influenced by budget constraints or shifts in project priorities that could affect the reopening of this position.

### 5. NEW INSIGHTS
- Consider **reaching out to the hiring manager** for insights into the closure and potential for reopening the position.
- Since the job is closed, it's crucial to assess whether any recent hires in similar roles could generate internal referrals for future openings.

This updated fact sheet aims to provide a clearer picture of the status and next steps for the closed job posting.

Please briefly explain what went wrong on this last run
(the root cause of the failure), and then come up with a new plan that takes steps and includes hints to overcome prior
challenges and especially avoids repeating the same mistakes. As before, the new plan should be concise, expressed in
bullet-point form, and consider the following team composition:

- ReqMaster: <no description, use name to identify the purpose of this participant>

### What Went Wrong
- The primary issue was attempting to source candidates for a job marked as **closed**, which led to wasted effort and misalignment with hiring needs.
- Lack of detailed insights into the reasons for closure and limited context about potential reopening created uncertainty in candidate sourcing efforts.

### New Plan
- **Validate Job Status**: Before sourcing, confirm the status of positions to ensure they are open. Utilize the ReqMaster to check job IDs and statuses in the system.
  
- **Investigate Reasons for Closure**: Conduct a follow-up with the hiring manager to understand the closure reasons and explore any potential for reopening. This insight can guide future efforts.

- **Look for Alternative Open Roles**: Instead of focusing on the closed position, identify similar open roles that align with the candidate profiles previously considered.

- **Establish Clear Communication Channels**: Set up regular check-ins with the hiring team to stay updated on job statuses and any changes in hiring needs.

- **Develop a Backup Plan**: In case of closed roles, have a pipeline of prospective candidates ready for potential openings in similar positions in the future.

- **Document Learnings**: Keep detailed notes on the reasons for job closures and any hiring changes to build a reference for future requisitions, allowing the team to adjust strategies accordingly.

By implementing these steps, we aim to streamline the process, avoid past mistakes, and enhance candidate sourcing effectiveness.
